arts.ttl: Latest SuperComputer predicts tight play-off battle for Reading come May
arts.sum: Reading are set to fight for, but miss out on, the play-offs, according to the latest SuperComputer predictions. Following Saturday's impressive 2-1 victory over Peterborough United, the Royals are firmly in the top 10 and could even end the week in the play-offs should Ruben Selles' side earn a positive result in Barnsley. With 15 matches of the season complete, and a hectic festive schedule to come in December, Reading are still fighting on three fronts with the EFL Trophy and FA Cup ties to come in the next fortnight.

arts.ttl: Four possible centre-back options for Reading at Barnsley with Amadou Mbengue out
arts.sum: Reading make the trip to promotion-chasing Barnsley this evening on the back off a 2-1 victory at Peterborough United, but they will have to do so without key defender Amadou Mbengue. Not only is the 22-year-old carrying a knock, which would have seen him miss the match regardless, the fan favourite is suspended after picking up his fifth yellow card at London Road. The Tykes, who lost to Wigan Athletic on Saturday, sit above the Royals on goal difference with both fighting for a top six position heading into December. With a thin squad as it is, the defensive line is set to get even younger at Oakwell with possible replacements for Mbengue. Take a look below at three possible options to replace the Senegalese youngster for the trip to South Yorkshire.

arts.ttl: Former Reading captain rounds off trio of Q&A sessions at Purple Turtle bar
arts.sum: Former Reading captain Jem Karacan returned to the town on Sunday for a Q&A event with fans at the Purple Turtle. Karacan, who played for the club in the Premier League and in the 2015 FA Cup semi-final defeat against Arsenal, talked about turning down Galatasary to join Reading as a 14-year-old and going on to sign pro at the same time as Hal Robson-Kanu, Simon Church and Gylfi Sugurdsson. After travelling down from Manchester for the event on Sunday morning, he also discussed the role that Eamonn Dolan played in his career - and how he coped with missing more than a year through injury during his time at Reading.

arts.ttl: Reading teenagers progress in cup as hit-and-miss form continues for Academy
arts.sum: Reading Under-18s have progressed into the knockout phase of the Premier League Under-18 Cup, finishing second in the group following a 4-1 victory at West Bromwich Albion. Mikele Leigertwood's side have struggled in the league, currently sat only above the Baggies in the table with eight points from nine matches. It is certainly not helped by the financial struggles of the first-team, with plenty of eligible teenagers skipping the youth side to become regulars in the Under-21 and first-team squads. Despite the struggles in the league, Blackburn Rovers and West Brom were defeated to ensure passage to the knockout phase of the Premier League Cup alongside Sunderland out of Group C.

arts.ttl: Fan Gallery: Over 1,200 Reading fans rewarded for braving Storm Bert with Posh win
arts.sum: Over 1,200 Reading supporters braved the elements thrown down by Storm Bert to travel to Cambridgeshire, but they were rewarded with an excellent performance and 2-1 victory over Peterborough United. Harvey Knibbs scored twice in the first-half and a resolute back line almost held on to a clean sheet, only conceding in the 96th-minute. Take a look below at our fan gallery, with pictures courtesy of JasonPIX.

arts.ttl: Reading boss on Mbengue and possible replacements at Barnsley after 'contact injury'
arts.sum: Reading boss Ruben Selles is expecting defender Amadou Mbengue to be out for 'some days' after limping off against Peterborough United, but insists he has plenty of options for Tuesday's trip to Barnsley. Harvey Knibbs netted a brace in the first-half and the Royals battled hard to keep the Posh out for 96 minutes, eventually conceding a consolation but holding out to win at London Road for the first time since 2001. Mbengue, who picked up his fifth yellow card and, in the process, will miss Tuesday's trip to Oakwell regardless of his fitness, will need replacing. Young Abraham Kanu was the substitute of choice for Selles in Peterborough, making his league debut and defending valiantly throughout.

arts.ttl: Preview: Barnsley vs. Reading - prediction, team news, lineups
arts.sum: Sports Mole previews Tuesday's League One clash between Barnsley and Reading, including predictions, team news and possible lineups.
